evals.prompt:
I've got three claims from my research that I need to verify against their sources. Can you submit them and then check them?

1. "OpenAI's GPT-4 was released in March 2023" - source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GPT-4 title: "GPT-4 Wikipedia"
2. "Anthropic was founded in 2021 by former OpenAI members" - source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anthropic title: "Anthropic Wikipedia"
3. "The transformer architecture was introduced in the paper Attention Is All You Need published in 2017" - source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transformer_(deep_learning_model) title: "Transformer Wikipedia"
evals.expected_output: Submit all 3 claims, verify against Wikipedia sources, present verification summary with co-browsing offer for any deviations.

assertions.name: workspace-initialized
assertions.description: cogni-claims/ directory created with claims.json, sources/, and history/ subdirectories

assertions.name: claims-created
assertions.description: All 3 claims appear in claims.json with unique claim-<uuid> IDs and correct statements

assertions.name: source-grouping
assertions.description: Claims grouped by URL for verification (3 unique URLs = 3 fetches, no duplicates)

assertions.name: verification-attempted
assertions.description: Evidence of WebFetch calls to all 3 Wikipedia URLs

assertions.name: summary-presented
assertions.description: Verification summary showing counts of verified, deviated, or source_unavailable claims

assertions.name: co-browse-offered
assertions.description: If deviations found, proactively offers to open the source in the browser for co-browsing

evals.prompt: what's the status of my claims? I submitted some earlier and want to see what needs attention
evals.expected_output: Recognize dashboard mode, render formatted dashboard with status sections and action hints.

files.description: Pre-populated claims registry with sample data

assertions.name: mode-inferred
assertions.description: Correctly interpreted 'what's the status' as dashboard mode without asking for clarification

assertions.name: grouped-by-status
assertions.description: Claims displayed grouped by status categories (deviated, verified, unverified, resolved)

assertions.name: severity-sorting
assertions.description: Deviated claims sorted by severity descending (critical first)

assertions.name: action-hints
assertions.description: Includes actionable next-step hints like /claims inspect and /claims resolve

assertions.name: deviation-context
assertions.description: Deviated claims include inline one-line summary of what the deviation is, not just the type label

assertions.name: formatting-quality
assertions.description: Dashboard uses markdown tables with claim IDs, truncated statements, and linked sources

evals.prompt: I have a claims.json with some deviated claims. Can you show me the details on the quantum computing claim and help me resolve it? The claim ID is claim-c3d4e5f6-a7b8-4c9d-0e1f-2a3b4c5d6e7f
evals.expected_output: Inspect the claim showing deviation details with What This Means section, then present resolution options.

files.description: Pre-populated claims registry with deviated quantum computing claim

assertions.name: correct-claim-found
assertions.description: Located the quantum computing claim by its ID and displayed the correct statement

assertions.name: deviation-details-shown
assertions.description: Shows deviation type (data_staleness), severity (medium), source excerpt, and explanation

assertions.name: what-this-means
assertions.description: Includes a labeled plain-language 'What this means' contextual explanation section

assertions.name: evidence-included
assertions.description: The verbatim source excerpt is shown before asking the user to make a resolution decision

assertions.name: all-five-options
assertions.description: All 5 resolution options presented: correct, dispute, alternative source, discard, accept as-is

assertions.name: suggested-correction
assertions.description: For the Correct option, a suggested corrected claim statement is provided based on what the source says

evals.prompt: I'm building a cogni-research plugin that produces sourced assertions. I need to submit these claims to the cogni-claims system for verification. What data format should I use? Show me the exact JSON structure.
evals.expected_output: Show ClaimRecord schema, batch submission format, required vs auto fields, and submission workflow.

assertions.name: claim-record-schema
assertions.description: Shows complete ClaimRecord JSON structure with all required fields (id, statement, source_url, source_title, submitted_by, status)

assertions.name: batch-format-shown
assertions.description: Includes the batch submission format with claims[] array and submitted_by field

assertions.name: field-requirements
assertions.description: Distinguishes between required fields (statement, source_url, source_title) and auto-generated fields (id, submitted_at, status)

assertions.name: submission-workflow
assertions.description: Explains how to invoke the cogni-claims:claims skill with mode submit for batch submission

assertions.name: id-format-correct
assertions.description: Specifies that claim IDs use the format claim-<uuid-v4>

evals.prompt:
I created this claim record for my plugin — can you check if it follows the cogni-claims contract?
```json
{"id": "abc-123", "text": "AI market growing fast", "url": "example.com", "by": "my-plugin", "status": "pending"}
```
evals.expected_output: Identify all schema violations: wrong ID format, wrong field names, wrong status value, missing source_title. Provide corrected example.

assertions.name: wrong-id-format
assertions.description: Flags abc-123 as wrong ID format (should be claim-<uuid-v4>)

assertions.name: wrong-field-names
assertions.description: Flags incorrect field names: text should be statement, url should be source_url, by should be submitted_by

assertions.name: wrong-status-value
assertions.description: Flags pending as an invalid status value (valid values: unverified, verified, deviated, source_unavailable, resolved)

assertions.name: missing-fields
assertions.description: Identifies missing required field source_title

assertions.name: corrected-example
assertions.description: Provides a corrected ClaimRecord JSON example matching the actual schema

evals.prompt: My source says 'revenue grew between 30-35% YoY' but the claim in my report says 'revenue grew 45% YoY'. What deviation type and severity should I assign? What should the DeviationRecord look like?
evals.expected_output: Identify misquotation type, high severity, provide complete valid DeviationRecord with hedged language.

assertions.name: correct-type
assertions.description: Identifies misquotation as the deviation type (claim misrepresents the source's stated figure)

assertions.name: correct-severity
assertions.description: Assigns high severity (significant misrepresentation — reader would form wrong conclusion about growth rate)

assertions.name: valid-record
assertions.description: Provides a DeviationRecord JSON with all required fields: id, claim_id, type, severity, source_excerpt, explanation, detected_at

assertions.name: hedged-language
assertions.description: Explanation uses epistemic humility language (appears to, suggests, may indicate) rather than definitive judgments
